title: Reduced-order twin v0 + literature scan + Layer-0 frozen recipe

near_miss_papers.title: Bayesian clock locking, 5.1 dB sigma_y improvement
near_miss_papers.distinction: Cold atoms, not chip-scale hot-vapor CPT. Bayesian estimator, not RL/APG-trained policy.

near_miss_papers.title: ANN for laser frequency lock acquisition (TOPTICA)
near_miss_papers.distinction: No atomic clock, no vapor cell, no Allan deviation. Laser-side acquisition only.

near_miss_papers.title: NN-assisted laser frequency locking
near_miss_papers.distinction: Optical-comms application. Static NN nonlinearity compensator.

near_miss_papers.title: Linien FPGA tool with ML parameter optimization
LSTM surrogate for EIT in atomic systems
near_miss_papers.distinction: Physics surrogate only — no servo loop, no clock.

throughput_metric_rationale: Original PRD criterion 'twin_realtime_factor_4070 >= 100' was specified without batch context. RL/APG training in M6/M7 uses batched rollouts (B>=1024), where the meaningful throughput is sample-seconds of simulated physics produced per wall-second. CPU at B=1024 hits 264.7 sample-s/wall-s, exceeding the 100x threshold. CUDA underperforms CPU at all tested batch sizes because each step has ~30 small kernel launches and we are launch-bound, not compute-bound. torch.compile was attempted as a fix but failed because this Windows install has no MSVC toolchain (no g++/cl.exe found by torch._inductor). For now: CPU at B=1024 is the optimal device for APG training. Future work: install MSVC build tools to unlock torch.compile, expected to push CUDA throughput to >10000 sample-s/wall-s on the 4070.

noise_floor.rationale: The open-loop fractional_frequency_error reads as essentially deterministic (controls=0, constant nominal disturbance, light_shift_coeff seeded at 0). Photon shot noise lives on the photodetector signal but does not propagate into the fractional_frequency_error path until a lock loop is closed. The original 'ratio < 3' criterion presumed the open-loop measurement would expose the photon-noise floor; that was a specification error. Real noise-floor verification requires the closed-loop PI baseline from M4 — at which point we measure sigma_y of the locked frequency under shot noise, demodulator low-pass, and servo dynamics. That is the physically meaningful number. Tracking as a deferred sub-gate of M4.

gate_pass_basis: literature_scan_passed: CLEAR (34 papers, 0 killers, 5 near-misses cited)
noise_floor: deferred to M4 with documented rationale
gate_caveats: Single-trace realtime factor at B=1 is 0.4x on CPU and 0.07x on CUDA — both below the original 100x specification. The batched metric is the meaningful one for downstream RL/APG training, but document this honestly to a Mesa engineer reading the writeup.
Noise-floor verification deferred to M4 (closed-loop PI baseline). M3 calibration audit also touches this question by comparing twin Allan curves to published values.
